channel.title: AI Nuggets
channel.description: Short deep dives on AI applied to biomedical research. Curated by Nigel 🦔 for Andrew Su.
channel.itunes:author: Nigel
channel.itunes:summary: Short deep dives on AI applied to biomedical research. Curated by Nigel for Andrew Su.

item.title: Cracking Open scGPT: Mechanistic Interpretability Extracts a Hematopoietic Algorithm from a Bio Foundation Model
item.description: Researchers apply mechanistic interpretability — the same techniques used to understand GPT internals — to scGPT, a single-cell foundation model, and extract a standalone hematopoietic algorithm from its attention weights. The extracted algorithm beats scVI, Palantir, and CellTypist in benchmarks while being 34.5x faster with ~1000x fewer parameters. Its four-factor core maps cleanly to T/lymphoid, B/plasma, granulocytic, and monocyte/macrophage gene programs — proving the model learned real biology, not just memorized patterns.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on the first extraction of a biologically useful algorithm from a foundation model via mechanistic interpretability, discovering a hematopoietic manifold inside scGPT.

item.title: Deep Dive: Synthetic Data for Cancer Research and Clinical Trials
item.description: A deep dive into the Nature Reviews Cancer review by Eckardt, Kather and colleagues on how generative AI can create synthetic patient data for cancer research. We cover the three big use cases — synthetic histopathology images, simulated clinical trials with synthetic control arms, and data democratization for rare cancers — plus the major unsolved challenges around bias amplification, privacy-utility tradeoffs, and the missing regulatory frameworks.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on how AI-generated synthetic data could transform cancer clinical trials and research data sharing, based on the Feb 2026 review in Nature Reviews Cancer.

item.title: CLEAR: When LLM Embeddings Meet Knowledge Graphs for Drug Repurposing
item.description: CLEAR fuses LLM embeddings with disease-specific biomedical knowledge graphs using attention-based graph learning, creating representations that encode both semantic meaning and relational structure. Tested on Alzheimer's and related dementias, it improves drug repurposing F1 scores by up to 30% over prior methods. A compelling proof that grounding language model representations in graph topology beats either approach alone.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on CLEAR, a framework that aligns LLM embeddings with biomedical knowledge graphs for drug repurposing in Alzheimer's and related dementias.

item.title: ChatSpatial: MCP-Powered AI Agents Tame the Spatial Transcriptomics Toolchain
item.description: ChatSpatial is an open-source agentic platform built on Model Context Protocol that unifies 60+ spatial transcriptomics methods across Python and R into a single conversational workflow. Instead of generating free-form code, the LLM selects from pre-validated tool schemas with domain-aware parameter inference, achieving near-deterministic reproducibility across seven LLM platforms. NIH-funded and open on GitHub.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on ChatSpatial, an MCP-based agentic system that orchestrates 60+ spatial transcriptomics tools across Python and R, eliminating the integration tax for researchers.

item.title: CiteAudit: Multi-Agent AI Catches Fake Citations Peer Review Misses
item.description: Hallucinated references — completely fabricated citations generated by LLMs — are passing peer review at top AI conferences. CiteAudit is a new open-source multi-agent system that uses five specialized AI agents to detect them with 97.2% accuracy, vastly outperforming commercial LLMs like GPT-5.2 which flag over 1,300 real citations as fake. Runs locally on Qwen 3 VL 235B, includes BioRxiv in its analysis, and processes 10 references in 2.3 seconds.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on CiteAudit, a multi-agent AI system that detects hallucinated citations in scientific papers — a growing threat to research integrity in the LLM era.

item.title: LocalCowork: Privacy-First AI Agents That Never Leave Your Laptop
item.description: Liquid AI releases LocalCowork, an open-source desktop AI agent powered by their 24B sparse MoE model that runs entirely on-device. With 75 local tools via MCP, sub-400ms latency, and zero cloud dependency, it's the first serious proof that useful AI agents for sensitive biomedical data don't need the cloud. Honest benchmarks included — 80% single-step accuracy, 26% multi-step.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on Liquid AI's LocalCowork — a fully local, privacy-first AI agent framework with MCP tool integration, and what it means for biomedical research computing.

item.title: DeepRare: AI Agents Team Up to End the Rare Disease Diagnostic Odyssey
item.description: A Nature paper introduces DeepRare, a multi-agent system using MCP-inspired architecture to diagnose rare diseases across 6,400+ cases and 2,919 conditions — nearly doubling the accuracy of the next-best method. What makes it special: traceable reasoning chains verified by physicians at 95.4% factual accuracy. A blueprint for agentic AI in clinical biomedicine.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on DeepRare, a multi-agent AI system for rare disease diagnosis with traceable reasoning, published in Nature.

item.title: GPT-5.4: The First AI That Operates Your Computer Better Than You Do
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 ships with native computer-use capabilities, scoring 75% on OSWorld — surpassing human performance at 72.4%. For computational biologists, this means AI agents that can actually drive bioinformatics software end-to-end, not just write code. A deep dive into what this means for scientific workflows.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on GPT-5.4's native computer-use capabilities and what they mean for computational biology workflows and scientific automation.

item.title: PerturbGen: Predicting How Gene Knockouts Reshape Cellular Trajectories
item.description: A new foundation model trained on 100M+ single-cell transcriptomes predicts how early genetic perturbations ripple through entire cellular trajectories — from immune responses to blood disorders to skin development. A deep dive into trajectory-aware perturbation modeling.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on PerturbGen, a generative foundation model that predicts how gene perturbations reshape cellular trajectories across time.

item.title: Evo2: The AI Learning to Write Genomes From Scratch
item.description: The Arc Institute's Evo2 — the largest DNA language model ever built — just published in Nature. It can generate entire bacterial genome sequences, but we're not at synthetic life yet. A deep dive into what it means for synthetic genomics and the evaluation gap ahead.
item.itunes:summary: Deep dive on Evo2, the Arc Institute's DNA language model that generated a complete bacterial genome inspired by Mycoplasma genitalium.
